reasoning: Comparing the historical mean daily temperatures at Le Puy-Loudes for November 9 (~6.4°C) and early February (~2.6°C), the expected mean temperature difference is approximately -3.8°C. Assuming daily average temperatures have a standard deviation of about 4.5°C for both times of year, the variance of the difference is roughly 40.5. A Z-score of roughly 0.6 corresponds to approximately a 28% chance that a random day in early February will be warmer than a random day on November 9. Without knowing the exact resolution date, I base this estimate on the question closed date in early February.
